project.name: claude-agents-dashboard
project.description: Live monitor for parallel Claude Code sessions — reads ~/.claude transcripts off disk (no daemon, no hooks) and shows per-session project, git branch, model, context usage, and current tool activity, polling every 3s.

nodes.name: App.tsx
nodes.summary: Root React component: renders the top-level SectionTabs switch and, based on the persisted section, shows the SessionsView or lazy-loads the Management chunk.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses React.lazy + Suspense to code-split the management view into its own chunk so the sessions bundle is unaffected.

nodes.name: App
nodes.summary: Top-level component holding persisted section state and conditionally rendering SessionsView vs. the lazily-loaded ManagementView.

nodes.name: MultiSelect.tsx
nodes.summary: Reusable toolbar facet component: a button summarizing the current selection that opens a checkbox popover, closing on outside click or Escape.
nodes.languageNotes: Self-contained with no external deps beyond React hooks; manages its own outside-click/Escape dismissal via document listeners.

nodes.name: MultiSelect
nodes.summary: Renders a multi-select popover facet control; tracks open/closed state, summarizes the selection, and toggles values in the onChange callback.

nodes.name: SectionTabs.tsx
nodes.summary: Top-level section switch UI (Sessions / Management / Analytics tabs) that reports the selected Section back to the parent via onChange.

nodes.name: SectionTabs
nodes.summary: Renders three tab buttons (Sessions, Management, Analytics) and invokes onChange with the clicked Section.

nodes.name: SessionsView.tsx
nodes.summary: The original live sessions monitor view: owns the 3s polling hook, persisted filter/sort view state, and composes Header, Toolbar, and SessionList.
nodes.languageNotes: Unmounting this component (by switching sections) stops the 3s poll since useSessions lives inside it.

nodes.name: SessionsView
nodes.summary: Fetches session data via useSessions, applies persisted view filters/sort via applyView, and renders Header, Toolbar, and SessionList.

nodes.name: Toolbar.tsx
nodes.summary: Filter and sort control bar with multi-select project/status facets, an activity-window select, and a sort-key/direction control; state is owned by the parent via onChange.

nodes.name: Toolbar
nodes.summary: Renders MultiSelect facets for projects/statuses plus native selects for activity window and sort key/direction, patching the View object on change.

nodes.name: useSessions.ts
nodes.summary: Custom hook that polls GET /api/sessions every 3 seconds and exposes the latest SessionsResponse plus a connected flag.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses an `alive` closure flag plus cleanup in the effect to avoid setting state after unmount.

nodes.name: useSessions
nodes.summary: Sets up a 3s interval that fetches /api/sessions, updating state with the response or flipping connected to false on failure.

nodes.name: filterSort.ts
nodes.summary: Pure filter/sort domain logic and shared constants for the Toolbar view: status labels/ordering, activity windows, the View type, and the applyView filter+sort function.
nodes.languageNotes: Exports both types (View, SortKey, ActivityWindow) and pure functions; no React or side effects, easing unit testing.

nodes.name: distinctProjects
nodes.summary: Returns the sorted set of unique project names present in a session list.

nodes.name: compare
nodes.summary: Ascending comparator between two sessions for a given sort key (tokens, name, status, or recency); caller flips sign for descending order.

nodes.name: applyView
nodes.summary: Filters sessions by selected projects, statuses, and activity window, then sorts the result using compare and the view's sort key/direction. Pure, no mutation.

nodes.name: main.tsx
nodes.summary: Client bootstrap entry point: mounts the App component into the DOM root under StrictMode and imports the global stylesheet.

nodes.name: Header.tsx
nodes.summary: Renders the dashboard title bar, active-session summary line, and the two account usage-limit bars (5h/weekly), falling back to a token-expired notice when the stored OAuth token is stale.

nodes.name: Header
nodes.summary: Top-level exported component that shows the generated timestamp, active-session count, running claude process count, and delegates to UsageExpired/UsageBars based on usageStatus.

nodes.name: UsageExpired
nodes.summary: Shows a plain 'token expired' hint in place of the usage bars when the stored OAuth token has expired; the CLI's own renewal flips the state back automatically.

nodes.name: UsageBars
nodes.summary: Filters the 5h and weekly rate-limit entries down to those with a known utilization and renders a UsageBar for each, or nothing if usage data is unavailable.

nodes.name: UsageBar
nodes.summary: Renders a single labeled progress bar for one rate-limit window, clamping utilization to 0-100%, color-coding by severity threshold, and showing a human-readable reset time.

nodes.name: DetailPane.tsx
nodes.summary: Right-hand pane of the Management three-pane layout; shows the selected config entry's metadata and delegates file rendering to HookDetail, MarkdownViewer, or FileViewer based on entry kind.

nodes.name: typeLabel
nodes.summary: Singularizes a group title like 'Skills' to 'skill' for display in the detail pane's type chip.

nodes.name: DetailPane
nodes.summary: Exported component that renders an empty state when nothing is selected, a HookDetail card for hook entries, or a generic metadata header plus MarkdownViewer/FileViewer for other entry kinds.

nodes.name: FileViewer.tsx
nodes.summary: Generic read-only file content viewer used across the Management section; fetches content via useFileContent, optionally pretty-prints JSON, and shows a truncation notice for large files.

nodes.name: prettyJson
nodes.summary: Attempts to re-parse and re-stringify JSON content with 2-space indentation for readability, falling back to the raw string on parse failure.

nodes.name: FileViewer
nodes.summary: Exported component that loads a file's content by path, shows loading/error states, and renders the content as monospace text (optionally pretty-printed) with a truncation notice when applicable.

nodes.name: HookDetail.tsx
nodes.summary: Structured card for a single Claude Code hook entry, showing event/matcher/source, the resolved script content, and a toggle to reveal the declaring settings/hooks file lazily.

nodes.name: HookDetail
nodes.summary: Exported component rendering a hook's event/matcher/source/command, an embedded FileViewer for its resolved script, and a lazily-mounted FileViewer for the declaring config file behind a show/hide toggle.

nodes.name: MarkdownViewer.tsx
nodes.summary: Markdown-aware file viewer that splits YAML frontmatter into a key/value table and renders the remaining body as plain monospace text.

nodes.name: MarkdownViewer
nodes.summary: Exported component that fetches a markdown file's content, parses its frontmatter via parseFrontmatter, and renders the extracted key/value pairs above the raw markdown body.

nodes.name: useManagement.ts
nodes.summary: Data-fetching hooks for the Management section: fetch-once index/project-scope hooks and a lazily-fetched, ref-cached file-content hook — deliberately unpolled since config changes rarely, unlike the 3s session poll.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses ref-held Maps as manual memoization caches alongside useState 'force update' counters to avoid re-fetching already-seen project scopes and file contents across selections.

nodes.name: useManagementIndex
nodes.summary: Fetches the global ManagementIndex from /api/management on mount or refreshKey change, tracking loading/error state and guarding against updates after unmount.

nodes.name: useProjectScope
nodes.summary: Lazily fetches a single project's ScopeConfig from /api/management/project, memoizing results per dirName+refreshKey in a ref-held Map to avoid re-fetching on re-selection.

nodes.name: useFileContent
nodes.summary: Lazily fetches a single file's content from /api/management/file by path, serving from a ref-held cache on repeat selections and tracking loading/error state.

nodes.name: format.ts
nodes.summary: Shared formatting utilities for the client UI: compact token counts, human-readable durations, relative 'time ago' strings, and reset-time labels.

nodes.name: fmtTok
nodes.summary: Formats a raw token/byte count into a compact string with M/K suffixes for large values.

nodes.name: fmtDuration
nodes.summary: Formats a millisecond duration into a short human-readable string (e.g. seconds/minutes/hours), guarding against non-finite input.

nodes.name: formatAgo
nodes.summary: Formats a millisecond age into a relative 'time ago' string used for session recency display.

nodes.name: formatResetTime
nodes.summary: Formats an ISO reset timestamp into a short, lowercase clock-time label, adding the date when the reset falls on a different day than today.

nodes.name: frontmatter.ts
nodes.summary: Zero-dependency, fail-open subset parser for YAML frontmatter blocks in Skill/agent markdown files, supporting key:value pairs, quoted strings, and folded/literal block scalars.
nodes.languageNotes: Deliberately tolerant: any malformed or non-frontmatter input returns the whole text as body rather than throwing, and nested maps/lists are silently skipped rather than parsed.

nodes.name: unquote
nodes.summary: Strips one matching pair of surrounding single or double quotes from a value string.

nodes.name: parseFrontmatter
nodes.summary: Parses a leading '---' delimited YAML frontmatter block into a flat key/value record plus the remaining body text, handling folded/literal block scalars and skipping nested structures; returns empty data with the whole text as body on any non-match.

nodes.name: SessionDetail.tsx
nodes.summary: Renders a session's expanded subagent panel — polls `useSessionDetail`, computes a wall-clock time range across agents, and lists each agent's type, description, duration, and tokens.

nodes.name: timeRange
nodes.summary: Computes the earliest start and latest end (or now, for still-running agents) across a list of AgentJob records to derive an overall elapsed duration.

nodes.name: agentMeta
nodes.summary: Builds the small metadata line (tokens, tool uses, elapsed/running duration) shown under each subagent row.

nodes.name: SessionDetail
nodes.summary: React component that expands a session row to show its live subagent list, polling status via useSessionDetail and rendering per-agent duration/token metadata.

nodes.name: SessionList.tsx
nodes.summary: Renders the ordered list of session rows, owning per-row expand/collapse state (which sessions show their SessionDetail panel).

nodes.name: SessionList
nodes.summary: Maps the sessions array to SessionRow components and tracks a Set of expanded session ids, toggling membership on row click.

nodes.name: SessionRow.tsx
nodes.summary: Renders a single session's summary row — status dot, project/branch/model, context usage bar, and last-active time — and conditionally renders SessionDetail when expanded.

nodes.name: SessionRow
nodes.summary: Presentational component for one session's row, formatting token/context usage and relative activity time, and delegating expand state to the onToggle callback.

nodes.name: useSessionDetail.ts
nodes.summary: Custom hook that fetches and polls `GET /api/sessions/:id` on a 3s interval, returning the SessionDetail payload for a single session's subagent activity.

nodes.name: useSessionDetail
nodes.summary: Fetches `/api/sessions/:id` on mount and every 3 seconds via setInterval, storing the latest SessionDetail in state and cleaning up the interval on unmount.

nodes.name: agents-cache.ts
nodes.summary: Incremental byte-offset cache over agents.ts's transcript parser: on repeated reads of the same growing transcript file, only the newly appended bytes are read and fed through the streaming line/event parser, instead of re-parsing the whole file.
nodes.languageNotes: Maintains a bounded LRU-ish Map keyed by file path, tracking byte offset + scan state per entry, with a leftover-buffer for partial trailing lines split across reads.

nodes.name: freshEntry
nodes.summary: Constructs a new empty cache entry (zero offset, fresh scan state, empty leftover buffer) for a transcript path not yet seen.

nodes.name: consumeLine
nodes.summary: Parses one buffered line as a JSON transcript record and folds its derived events into the running scan state, silently skipping malformed lines.

nodes.name: evictIfNeeded
nodes.summary: Evicts the oldest cache entry once the cache exceeds its size cap, keeping memory bounded across many concurrently-tracked transcripts.

nodes.name: readAgentsCached
nodes.summary: Main cache entry point: stats the transcript file, detects truncation/rotation, reads only the bytes appended since the last call, streams them through consumeLine, and returns the resulting AgentJob list — falling back to a full readAgents() parse on cache-miss edge cases.

nodes.name: _resetAgentsCache
nodes.summary: Test-only helper that clears the module-level cache Map between test cases.

nodes.name: _agentsCacheSize
nodes.summary: Test-only helper exposing the current number of cached entries, used to assert eviction behavior.

nodes.name: agents.ts
nodes.summary: Whole-file subagent parser: a pure event parser (parseRecordEvents) plus a reducer (applyEvent/toAgentJobs) that turns a raw transcript's Task tool_use/tool_result/notification records into a list of AgentJob objects with duration, tokens, and tool-use counts.
nodes.languageNotes: Splits parsing (stateless, testable event extraction) from reduction (stateful Map-based pairing of launch/result events by tool_use_id and agent id) so agents-cache.ts can drive the same reducer incrementally.

nodes.name: isAgentLaunch
nodes.summary: Checks whether a content block is a Task tool_use invocation, i.e. a subagent launch.

nodes.name: contentText
nodes.summary: Extracts plain text from a message's content, whether it is a raw string or an array of content blocks (delegating tool_result blocks to toolResultText).

nodes.name: toolResultText
nodes.summary: Flattens a tool_result block's content array into one text string, joining individual text sub-blocks.

nodes.name: finiteOrNull
nodes.summary: Normalizes a value to a finite number or null, guarding downstream arithmetic against NaN/undefined.

nodes.name: intFromMatch
nodes.summary: Runs a regex against text and parses the first capture group as an integer, used to pull duration/token/tool-use counts out of notification `<usage>` blocks.

nodes.name: parseRecordEvents
nodes.summary: Pure function that inspects one raw transcript record (user/assistant message) and emits zero or more typed events (agent-launched, agent-result, agent-async-update) describing subagent lifecycle activity found in it — the parsing half of the parser/reducer split.

nodes.name: createScanState
nodes.summary: Creates the empty mutable scan state (launches array, byToolUseId map, byAgentId map) that applyEvent folds records into.

nodes.name: applyEvent
nodes.summary: Reducer that folds one parsed event into the scan state — recording new launches, pairing tool_results back to their launch by tool_use_id, and applying async notification updates keyed by agent id.

nodes.name: toAgentJobs
nodes.summary: Converts the accumulated scan state into the final newest-first AgentJob[] list, computing derived duration from timestamps when the transcript itself didn't report one.

nodes.name: readAgents
nodes.summary: Full non-incremental entry point: reads an entire transcript file from disk, splits it into lines, runs each through parseRecordEvents/applyEvent, and returns the resulting AgentJob list — the fallback path agents-cache.ts calls when incremental reading isn't viable.

nodes.name: analyze.ts
nodes.summary: Deterministic whole-session post-mortem analyzer for the /doctor skill: reads a transcript once and computes main-agent token totals, per-turn stats, per-tool usage/errors, subagent aggregates, and heuristic error/rework signals into a SessionAnalysis.
nodes.languageNotes: Deliberately separates 'facts' (this file) from 'judgment' (the doctor skill's write-up) — every field here is a deterministic computation over the transcript, no LLM-derived scoring.

nodes.name: num
nodes.summary: Coerces a possibly-missing numeric field to a finite number, defaulting to 0.

nodes.name: toolResultText
nodes.summary: Flattens a tool_result content block into plain text, mirroring the same-named helper in agents.ts but scoped locally to this analyzer.

nodes.name: isErrorResult
nodes.summary: Determines whether a tool_result block represents an error, checking the is_error flag and the `<tool_use_error>` text marker.

nodes.name: userText
nodes.summary: Extracts a user message's plain text content, used to scan for correction-keyword matches.

nodes.name: analyzeSession
nodes.summary: Top-level analyzer: reads a transcript, walks every record accumulating token totals, per-turn max/avg, per-tool duration/errors/approx-tokens, pending-tool pairing for error/retry detection, user-correction keyword hits, and merges in subagent totals from readAgents(), returning the full SessionAnalysis.

nodes.name: types.ts
nodes.summary: The API contract shared by server and client: every request/response shape (SessionsResponse, SessionDetail, ManagementIndex, AnalyticsReport, ScopeConfig, etc.) as pure TypeScript interfaces/types, imported by both sides with no runtime coupling.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses `import type` semantics implicitly (pure type-only file with zero imports/exports of values) so it compiles away entirely at runtime — the single source of truth the CLAUDE.md calls out for adding any new API field.

nodes.name: api.ts
nodes.summary: HTTP handler layer for the dashboard's REST API: serves session listings, per-session subagent detail, the read-only management (config browser) endpoints, and analytics report endpoints, delegating to the scan/usage/management/analytics-store modules for actual data.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Node HTTP server entry point: loads config, routes /api/sessions and /api/management requests to api.ts handlers, static-serves the built client in production, and auto-opens the browser.

nodes.name: config.ts
nodes.summary: Loads dashboard runtime configuration by merging process.env, a project .env file, and hardcoded defaults (precedence: process.env > .env > defaults), including Docker auto-detection for disabling the process-liveness scan.

nodes.name: management.ts
nodes.summary: Read-only scanner for Claude Code configuration across global (~/.claude, including installed plugins) and per-project (.claude/*) scopes, producing ScopeConfig data (skills, agents, commands, rules, hooks, memory, settings) and a security allowlist for safely serving individual config files.
nodes.languageNotes: Deliberately avoids prefix/subtree path checks for the file-serving security boundary; instead builds an explicit allowlist Set from everything it itself enumerated, since ~/.claude also holds sensitive files like .credentials.json.

nodes.name: serveSessions
nodes.summary: Handles GET /api/sessions: runs scanSessions, optionally attaches usage-limit bars, and writes the JSON SessionsResponse, falling back to an empty-but-valid response on scan errors.

nodes.name: serveSessionDetail
nodes.summary: Handles GET /api/sessions/:id: validates the session id, locates its transcript, and returns the on-demand parsed subagent job list via readAgentsCached.

nodes.name: serveManagementIndex
nodes.summary: Handles GET /api/management: concurrently reads the global config scope and the list of recently-active projects, returning the combined ManagementIndex.

nodes.name: serveManagementProject
nodes.summary: Handles GET /api/management/project: resolves a dirName against the enumerated recent-projects list and returns that single project's ScopeConfig.

nodes.name: serveManagementFile
nodes.summary: Handles GET /api/management/file: enforces the collectServablePaths() security allowlist before reading and returning a single config file's content.

nodes.name: serveAnalytics
nodes.summary: Handles GET /api/analytics: lists previously generated analytics reports via the analytics-store module.

nodes.name: serveAnalyticsInspect
nodes.summary: Handles GET /api/analytics/:id: validates the id and generates/returns a specific analytics report.

nodes.name: serveStatic
nodes.summary: Serves a static file from client/dist for production mode, guarding against path traversal by checking the resolved path stays within the dist directory.

nodes.name: parseEnv
nodes.summary: Parses a .env-format text blob into a key/value record, handling comments, blank lines, and quoted values.

nodes.name: isDockerContainer
nodes.summary: Detects whether the process is running inside a Docker container by checking for /.dockerenv, used to default skipProcScan to true since lsof-based liveness detection can't see host processes from inside a container.

nodes.name: loadConfig
nodes.summary: Builds the final Config object by merging process.env, a parsed .env file, and DEFAULTS with strict precedence, applying Docker auto-detection for skipProcScan.

nodes.name: readSkillsDir
nodes.summary: Reads a skills directory, parsing each skill's frontmatter metadata via parseFrontmatter into ManagementEntry items.

nodes.name: readMdDir
nodes.summary: Recursively reads a directory of markdown-based config items (agents, commands, rules), parsing frontmatter and descending into subdirectories up to a depth limit.

nodes.name: resolveScriptPath
nodes.summary: Resolves a hook command string to an absolute script path, restricting the result to a set of allowed root directories for safety.

nodes.name: readHooksConfig
nodes.summary: Parses a hooks configuration object (from settings.json or a plugin's hooks.json) into a flat list of ManagementEntry hook items, resolving each hook's script path.

nodes.name: readPlugin
nodes.summary: Reads a single installed plugin's full subtree (skills, agents, commands, rules, hooks) given its install path, tagging every item with the plugin name.

nodes.name: readPlugins
nodes.summary: Reads installed_plugins.json and expands each entry into a full plugin ScopeConfig subtree via readPlugin.

nodes.name: memoryItems
nodes.summary: Filters a list of candidate memory file paths (e.g. CLAUDE.md variants) down to those that actually exist, producing ManagementEntry items.

nodes.name: settingsHooks
nodes.summary: Reads settings.json and settings.local.json in a directory and extracts their hooks via readHooksConfig, deduping across both files.

nodes.name: readGlobalScope
nodes.summary: Assembles the full global ScopeConfig (~/.claude skills/agents/commands/rules/hooks/memory/settings plus every installed plugin's subtree, merged in) for the Management tab's Global scope.

nodes.name: readProjectScope
nodes.summary: Assembles a single project's ScopeConfig from its .claude/* directory plus the project root CLAUDE.md, tagging every item as 'project'.

nodes.name: listRecentProjects
nodes.summary: Derives the list of recently-active project directories from session transcript cwds (same lookback window as session scanning), deduped by cwd and sorted newest-first.

nodes.name: resolveProject
nodes.summary: Resolves a dirName to its matching recent ProjectRef by membership lookup, returning null if not found among recently-active projects.

nodes.name: collectServablePaths
nodes.summary: Builds the exact allowlist Set of file paths safe to serve via the management file endpoint, derived from everything the global and per-project scope scans themselves enumerated — the core security invariant for serveManagementFile.

nodes.name: readServableFile
nodes.summary: Reads a single file's content after verifying it is present in the allowed-paths Set, capping the returned content at FILE_CONTENT_CAP bytes and flagging truncation.

nodes.name: scan.ts
nodes.summary: Enumerates Claude Code session transcripts under ~/.claude/projects, computes each session's status (working/incomplete/idle/question) via transcript signals, and applies the process-liveness gate (lsof-based) to force stale/dead sessions to idle.
nodes.languageNotes: liveCwds() shells out to `lsof -c claude -a -d cwd -Fn` as a fail-open liveness probe; granularity is per-cwd (not per-session) since the claude CLI exposes no session id in argv/env.

nodes.name: transcript.ts
nodes.summary: Tail-reads the last chunk of a session's .jsonl transcript file to derive token usage, context window sizing, model, current tool activity, and turn-completion/question signals used to compute session status.
nodes.languageNotes: Only reads the last DEFAULT_TAIL_BYTES of the file via fs.readSync at a computed offset, avoiding a full-file read for potentially large transcripts.

nodes.name: usage.ts
nodes.summary: Fetches and caches account-level 5h/weekly Anthropic rate-limit usage from the private oauth/usage endpoint, reading the OAuth token from the macOS keychain (falling back to ~/.claude/.credentials.json), with fully fail-open error handling and a synchronous cache with non-blocking background refresh.
nodes.languageNotes: getCachedUsageState() is synchronous by design so the 3s /api/sessions poll never blocks on a network call; it triggers a fire-and-forget refreshNow() when the cache is older than 60s.

nodes.name: vite.config.ts
nodes.summary: Vite dev server configuration for the client: proxies /api to the backend port (reusing the backend's own loadConfig() so the port stays in sync), binds all network interfaces for LAN/phone access, and logs the LAN IP on server start.

nodes.name: listTranscripts
nodes.summary: Enumerates every .jsonl transcript file under ~/.claude/projects/*/, returning path, project directory, session id, and mtime for each.

nodes.name: countClaudeProcesses
nodes.summary: Counts running `claude` CLI processes via ps/grep, used as a coarse indicator surfaced in the scan output.

nodes.name: liveCwds
nodes.summary: Shells out to `lsof -c claude -a -d cwd -Fn` to build the set of working directories with a live claude CLI process attached; fail-open (returns null) on any lsof error, timeout, or absence.
nodes.languageNotes: The case-sensitive -c claude filter deliberately excludes the capital-C Claude.app GUI shell, scoping the liveness gate to CLI sessions only.

nodes.name: scanSessions
nodes.summary: Top-level session scan: lists recent transcripts, reads each one, computes status via the 2x2 recent/turnComplete matrix plus the question override, applies the process-liveness gate to force dead sessions idle, and filters out empty (no-message) sessions.

nodes.name: readTail
nodes.summary: Reads only the last tailBytes of a file via a computed offset and fs.readSync, avoiding a full read of potentially very large transcript files.

nodes.name: usageTokens
nodes.summary: Extracts the input/output/cache token counts from a transcript record's usage block.

nodes.name: resolveWindow
nodes.summary: Determines the effective context window size (standard vs large) for a session based on the model name and an env override pattern list.

nodes.name: describeTool
nodes.summary: Builds a short human-readable description of the tool currently in use (e.g. subagent type/description, command, file path, or pattern) for display as current activity.

nodes.name: readTranscript
nodes.summary: Main transcript reader: tails the file, walks records backward to find the newest conversational message, and derives model, tokens, context window, turnComplete, waitingOnQuestion, lastMessageTs, and current tool activity signals.

nodes.name: readToken
nodes.summary: Reads the Anthropic OAuth access token from the macOS keychain, falling back to ~/.claude/.credentials.json; returns an expired/ok/unavailable state without ever refreshing the token.

nodes.name: tokenFromCredsBlob
nodes.summary: Parses a claudeAiOauth credentials JSON blob into a token state, checking the expiresAt timestamp against the current time.

nodes.name: mapUsage
nodes.summary: Maps the Anthropic usage API's raw response payload (defensively handling both a top-level shape and a rate_limits-wrapped shape) into the app's UsageLimits type.

nodes.name: fetchUsage
nodes.summary: Makes the raw HTTPS/HTTP request to the private Anthropic oauth/usage endpoint with the given token, parsing and mapping the JSON response; fails open (returns null) on any network or parse error.

nodes.name: refreshNow
nodes.summary: Performs a full token-read plus fetchUsage cycle and updates the module-level cache and status, called both eagerly and as a fire-and-forget background refresh.

nodes.name: getCachedUsageState
nodes.summary: Synchronously returns the last cached usage state and triggers a non-blocking refreshNow() if the cache is older than 60s, so the frequent /api/sessions poll never blocks on network I/O.

nodes.name: logHostLanIp
nodes.summary: Logs the machine's LAN IP address once the Vite dev server starts, so a phone on the same wifi can find the Network URL without a tunnel.

nodes.name: ItemList.tsx
nodes.summary: Middle pane of the Management view: a filter box plus entries grouped by type, with collapsible group/sub-group headers whose collapse state persists to localStorage.
nodes.languageNotes: Collapse state is keyed by 'title/subgroup' strings so a group's collapsed/expanded state survives scope switches; default collapse state is a function of group/subgroup identity rather than stored per-item.

nodes.name: ManagementView.tsx
nodes.summary: Top-level container for the read-only Management section: wires ScopeMenu, ItemList, and DetailPane into a three-pane layout backed by useManagementIndex/useProjectScope, with persisted scope selection and derived (non-effect-based) selection/fallback logic.
nodes.languageNotes: Default export, loaded via React.lazy elsewhere so the Sessions bundle is unaffected; stale selection state (dead scope or dead selected key) is resolved by derivation during render rather than via effects.

nodes.name: ScopeMenu.tsx
nodes.summary: Left pane of the Management view listing 'Global' plus recently-active projects (newest-first) for scope selection.

nodes.name: usePersistedState.ts
nodes.summary: Generic useState replacement backed by localStorage: lazily reads and parses the stored value once, writes JSON on every change, and fails open (falls back to the default) on missing/bad JSON or a throwing localStorage.
nodes.languageNotes: Object-shaped values are shallow-merged over the fallback default so a value persisted by an older release still gains newly-added fields.

nodes.name: managementEntries.ts
nodes.summary: Pure normalization layer that transforms a ScopeConfig into flat, selectable EntryGroup/Entry structures for the Management item list, plus a case-insensitive filter over those groups.
nodes.languageNotes: Discriminated union `Entry` type ('file' | 'hook') lets the UI branch on kind while sharing common fields; sort/group ordering is fixed by design (local sources before plugin subgroups, alphabetical within).

nodes.name: collapsedByDefault
nodes.summary: Determines whether a sub-group should start collapsed: Hooks groups and user/project sub-groups start open, everything else (plugin sub-groups) starts collapsed.

nodes.name: badgeText
nodes.summary: Strips the 'plugin:' prefix from a badge string for display, since the sub-group header already conveys the plugin context.

nodes.name: ItemList
nodes.summary: Renders the filter input and the grouped/collapsible entry list; manages toggle state via usePersistedState and derives which groups/sub-groups are visible based on filter and collapse state.

nodes.name: ManagementView
nodes.summary: Default-exported component that composes ScopeMenu, ItemList, and DetailPane, resolving scope, config, entry groups, and the currently selected entry from persisted and fetched state.

nodes.name: ScopeMenu
nodes.summary: Renders the 'Global' entry plus a list of recent project entries as clickable scope-selection rows.

nodes.name: usePersistedState
nodes.summary: Generic hook returning a [value, setValue] pair whose value is lazily initialized from localStorage and persisted back to localStorage on every change, failing open on errors.

nodes.name: fileKind
nodes.summary: Infers a FileKind ('markdown' | 'json' | 'text') from a file path's extension for detail-pane rendering hints.

nodes.name: itemSubgroup
nodes.summary: Strips the 'plugin:' prefix from a source string to derive the sub-group name used for grouping entries.

nodes.name: bySubgroupThenLabel
nodes.summary: Comparator ordering entries with user/project sub-groups first, then plugin sub-groups alphabetically, then labels alphabetically within each sub-group.

nodes.name: fromItems
nodes.summary: Converts a list of ConfigItem into file-kind Entry objects, optionally computing and sorting by sub-group.

nodes.name: fromHooks
nodes.summary: Converts a list of HookInfo into hook-kind Entry objects sorted by event name, generating unique keys and labels.

nodes.name: buildEntries
nodes.summary: Builds the fixed-order list of EntryGroups (Plugins, Skills, Agents, Commands, Rules, Hooks, Memory, Settings) from a ScopeConfig, including the global-only Plugins group.

nodes.name: filterEntries
nodes.summary: Filters entry groups down to entries whose label or sublabel case-insensitively matches the query, dropping groups left empty; returns groups unchanged for an empty query.

nodes.name: .dockerignore
nodes.summary: Excludes node_modules, build output, VCS metadata, IDE files, env files, logs, and OS artifacts from the Docker build context to keep image builds fast and avoid leaking secrets.

nodes.name: docker-compose.dev.yml
nodes.summary: Development Compose file that builds the Dockerfile's `dev` stage, exposes Vite (5173) and the API (4173), bind-mounts the repo for HMR, and mounts the host's `~/.claude` read-only plus OAuth creds for usage bars.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses an anonymous volume (`/app/node_modules`) to prevent the source bind-mount from shadowing the container's Linux-built node_modules — a common pnpm/Docker dev pattern.

nodes.name: docker-compose.yml
nodes.summary: Production Compose file that builds the default (runtime) Dockerfile target, exposes port 4173, and mounts the host's `~/.claude` directory read-only so the dashboard can scan real session transcripts inside the container.

nodes.name: Dockerfile
nodes.summary: Multi-stage Docker build with four targets: `deps` (installs pnpm dependencies once), `dev` (bind-mount dev server for docker-compose.dev.yml), `build` (bundles the Vite client to client/dist), and `runtime` (minimal production image running the server via tsx with no compile step).
nodes.languageNotes: Multi-stage build lets the `dev` and `runtime` targets both reuse the `deps` stage's installed node_modules, while `runtime` also pulls only the compiled client assets from `build` — keeping the final image free of devDependencies and source not needed at runtime.

nodes.name: deps
nodes.summary: Base stage on node:20-alpine that enables corepack and installs pnpm dependencies with a frozen lockfile, reused by both the dev and build stages.

nodes.name: dev
nodes.summary: Development stage extending `deps`; expects the source tree to be bind-mounted (per docker-compose.dev.yml) and runs `pnpm dev`, exposing Vite (5173) and the API (4173).

nodes.name: build
nodes.summary: Build stage on node:20-alpine that copies installed node_modules from `deps`, adds the full source, and runs `pnpm build` to bundle the client into client/dist.

nodes.name: runtime
nodes.summary: Final production stage on node:20-alpine that copies node_modules from `deps`, the server and shared source, and the bundled client from `build`; sets NODE_ENV=production and runs the server via tsx on port 4173.

nodes.name: .env.example
nodes.summary: Template for the dashboard's runtime environment variables — port, session count/lookback/activity-window thresholds, the Docker process-scan skip flag, and an optional context-window override — with defaults documented inline for copying to .env.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Project overview and user-facing documentation covering setup (dev/production/Docker), testing, the full feature set (session rows, status dot semantics, subagent detail, toolbar, usage bars), configuration variables, layout, and a list of not-yet-implemented features.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: Node package manifest defining the project's npm scripts (dev, dev:docker, build, start, test, typecheck, doctor), its two runtime dependencies (React 18), dev tooling (Vite, tsx, TypeScript, concurrently), and pnpm/engine constraints.

nodes.name: skills-lock.json
nodes.summary: Lockfile pinning two vendored Claude Code skills (frontend-design, vercel-react-best-practices) to their GitHub source paths and content hashes for reproducible skill installation.

nodes.name: tsconfig.json
nodes.summary: TypeScript compiler configuration in strict, noEmit mode targeting ES2022 with bundler module resolution and React JSX support, scoped to the shared/server/client-src/test source directories.
nodes.languageNotes: noEmit + moduleResolution: Bundler reflects that the server runs via tsx (no compile step) and the client is bundled by Vite — tsc here is purely a type-checker, not a build tool.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Primary project instructions and architecture reference for the Claude Agents Dashboard: describes the FE/BE split, the session-status state machine, usage-limit fetching, the read-only Management config browser, the Analytics section, view persistence, and general conventions/gotchas.
nodes.languageNotes: Checked-in project CLAUDE.md that overrides default agent behavior; organized as a living architecture doc with dedicated sections per subsystem (sessions, usage limits, management, analytics, persistence).

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Vite HTML entry point for the React client — mounts the app into a `#root` div and loads `src/main.tsx` as an ES module.

nodes.name: styles.css
nodes.summary: Global stylesheet for the dashboard UI: defines the dark-theme CSS custom properties (colors, fonts) and styling for tabs, toolbar, usage bars, session list, and other layout primitives, ported verbatim from the original inline renderPage() markup.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses CSS custom properties (`:root` variables) for a centralized dark color palette; class names are kept stable intentionally to match a prior inline-HTML implementation.

nodes.name: agent-tracking-cache.md
nodes.summary: Design idea document proposing an incremental byte-offset cache for subagent tracking, covering the correctness trap of cache checkpoints, a proposed offset-following design, caveats, and the core tradeoff to decide before building.
nodes.languageNotes: This idea was later implemented as server/lib/agents-cache.ts, referenced in the project CLAUDE.md.

nodes.name: session-and-agent-tracking.md
nodes.summary: Learning/onboarding doc explaining how the dashboard tracks sessions and subagents from on-disk transcript files: the two read paths (256KB tail vs. whole-file walk), session enumeration/ranking, the session-status state machine, and the lsof liveness gate.

nodes.name: 2026-07-06-usage-token-refresh-removal.md
nodes.summary: Removal plan documenting why the in-app OAuth token refresh ('Sync' button) feature was pulled out, what was removed, the reasoning (too much machinery, incompatible with Docker), current post-removal behavior, and guidance for a future platform-independent approach.

nodes.name: 2026-07-01-usage-token-refresh.md
nodes.summary: Detailed multi-task implementation plan (later reverted) for adding OAuth token refresh to the usage-bar feature: covers the API contract in shared/types.ts, status plumbing in server/lib/usage.ts, a new server/lib/token-refresh.ts spawn/single-flight module, a POST /api/usage/refresh endpoint, a phantom-session filter in scan.ts, and client-side Sync button/expired-state UI, ending with end-to-end verification steps.
nodes.languageNotes: Very long, task-by-task implementation plan; the associated feature was ultimately removed (see docs/plans/2026-07-06-usage-token-refresh-removal.md), making this a historical/superseded planning artifact.

nodes.name: 2026-07-01-usage-token-refresh-design.md
nodes.summary: Design spec for the usage-bar token refresh feature: states the problem and goal, the chosen decision, a component-by-component design (types contract, server status plumbing, refresh endpoint, phantom-session mitigation, client Header changes, tests), accepted trade-offs, and explicit out-of-scope items.
nodes.languageNotes: Companion spec to the longer implementation plan in docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-01-usage-token-refresh.md; both describe a feature that was later removed per docs/plans/2026-07-06-usage-token-refresh-removal.md.

nodes.name: projectsRoot
nodes.summary: Returns the default ~/.claude/projects transcripts root, optionally under a supplied home directory override.

nodes.name: decodeProjectName
nodes.summary: Best-effort human label for a project when no cwd is available: decodes the Claude Code directory name (-a-b-c -> a/b/c) and takes the basename; lossy fallback only.

nodes.name: normCwd
nodes.summary: Strips a trailing slash so cwd strings compare consistently, used by the process-liveness gate.

nodes.name: claudeHome
nodes.summary: Returns the ~/.claude directory path, optionally under a supplied home directory override.

nodes.name: settingsInfo
nodes.summary: Builds SettingsFileInfo entries for settings.json and settings.local.json in a directory, noting which of the two actually exist.

nodes.name: toPosInt
nodes.summary: Coerces a value to a positive integer, falling back to a default when parsing fails or the result isn't positive.

nodes.name: toBool
nodes.summary: Coerces a value to a boolean, accepting false/0/no/off and true/1/yes/on case-insensitively, else falls back to a default.

nodes.name: windowLabel
nodes.summary: Formats a numeric context-window size into a short human label (e.g. '1M', '200k').

nodes.name: baseUrl
nodes.summary: Resolves the base URL for the Anthropic usage endpoint, always defaulting to api.anthropic.com and only overridable via the test-only CLAUDE_USAGE_BASE_URL env var (deliberately not ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL/CLAUDE_CODE_API_BASE_URL, which point at inference proxies with no such route).

nodes.name: renderGroup
nodes.summary: Inner helper closed over ItemList's collapse state: renders one EntryGroup's rows, inserting collapsible sub-group headers and skipping rows under a collapsed sub-group.
nodes.languageNotes: Defined as a local const inside the ItemList component (not a module-level export), closing over `toggled`/`toggle`/`filtered`/`selectedKey`.

layers.name: Client (React UI)
layers.description: The Vite + React + TypeScript frontend — session monitor and Management config-browser views, their supporting components, hooks, and pure UI helpers.

layers.name: Server (Node Backend)
layers.description: The zero-runtime-dependency Node/tsx backend — HTTP entry point and the /api/sessions + /api/management route handlers, plus the lib/ services for transcript scanning, subagent parsing, config loading, usage-limit fetching, and the config-browser scanner.

layers.name: Shared API Contract
layers.description: The typed boundary crossing FE/BE — shared/types.ts (every request/response shape) and shared/frontmatter.ts (the YAML-frontmatter parser reused by both server scanning and client markdown rendering); imported everywhere, imports nothing.

layers.name: Infrastructure
layers.description: Docker build/runtime topology for the dashboard: the multi-stage Dockerfile (deps/dev/build/runtime targets), dev and production Compose files, and the Docker build-context ignore rules.

layers.name: Configuration
layers.description: Project-level build and environment configuration: package manifest, TypeScript compiler settings, Vite dev-server proxy config, the vendored-skills lockfile, and the runtime .env template.

layers.name: Documentation
layers.description: User- and contributor-facing docs: the project README, the primary CLAUDE.md architecture reference, and the docs/ideas, docs/learning, and docs/plans/specs write-ups covering session tracking and the usage-token-refresh feature history.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: The README explains what this dashboard is: a live monitor for parallel Claude Code sessions that reads transcript files straight off disk under ~/.claude/projects, with no daemon and no hooks required. It also introduces the three-domain monolith split — a Node backend, a React frontend, and a shared API contract — that the rest of this tour will walk through, plus the newer read-only Management config-browser tab.

tour.title: Backend Entry Point
tour.description: server/index.ts is the Node HTTP server's entry point — it loads runtime configuration, wires up routes for /api/sessions and /api/management, static-serves the built client in production, and auto-opens a browser. This is where the backend half of the monolith actually starts running, and the top of the BFS dependency chain the next few steps will follow outward.

tour.title: The API Route Handlers
tour.description: server/api.ts is the HTTP handler layer index.ts delegates to: it serves session listings, per-session subagent detail, the Management config-browser endpoints, and analytics reports. Its high fan-out (6 downstream modules) makes it the traffic hub of the backend — every request effectively passes through here before reaching a specialized lib/ module.

tour.title: The Shared API Contract
tour.description: shared/types.ts is the single most depended-upon file in the whole codebase — fan-in of 20, dwarfing everything else. It defines every request/response shape (SessionsResponse, SessionDetail, ManagementIndex, ScopeConfig) as plain TypeScript interfaces with zero runtime code, forming the one typed boundary the frontend and backend both import but never share logic across. Understanding this file means understanding the vocabulary the rest of the tour speaks in.
tour.languageLesson: TypeScript interfaces with no implementation compile away entirely — importing shared/types.ts from both client and server costs nothing at runtime, only structural type-checking at build time. This is how the project achieves a typed FE/BE boundary with 'zero runtime coupling.'

tour.title: Configuration Loading
tour.description: server/lib/config.ts merges process.env, a project .env file, and hardcoded defaults (in that precedence order) into the runtime configuration server/index.ts loads at startup. It also auto-detects when it's running inside a Docker container to disable the process-liveness scan from the next step, since that scan can't see host processes from inside a container's namespace.

tour.title: Reading Sessions Off Disk
tour.description: server/lib/scan.ts is the heart of the live-monitor feature: it enumerates every session transcript under ~/.claude/projects, computes each session's status (working/incomplete/idle/question) from transcript signals, and applies an lsof-based liveness gate to force stale or dead sessions to idle. server/lib/transcript.ts supplies the raw signals it depends on, tail-reading just the last chunk of each .jsonl file to derive tokens, model, context window, and turn-completion state — a deliberately cheap read given scan.ts runs on every 3-second poll.

tour.title: Subagent Tracking
tour.description: Beyond top-level session status, the dashboard can drill into a session's subagent activity. server/lib/agents.ts is a pure event parser plus reducer that turns a transcript's Task tool_use/tool_result records into AgentJob objects with duration, tokens, and tool-use counts; server/lib/agents-cache.ts wraps it with an incremental byte-offset cache so repeated reads of a growing transcript only process newly appended bytes instead of re-parsing the whole file each time.
tour.languageLesson: Splitting a pure parser/reducer (agents.ts) from a caching wrapper (agents-cache.ts) keeps the parsing logic unit-testable without any filesystem or byte-offset state — the cache only decides which bytes to feed the parser, never how to interpret them.

tour.title: Account Usage Limits
tour.description: server/lib/usage.ts fetches the account's 5-hour and weekly Anthropic rate-limit utilization from a private oauth/usage endpoint, reading the OAuth token from the macOS keychain (falling back to a credentials file). It's fully fail-open — no token, an expired token, or a network error all just result in the header's usage bars being omitted rather than the dashboard breaking — and it caches synchronously so the 3-second session poll is never blocked waiting on a network call.

tour.title: Frontend Entry Point
tour.description: Crossing from the Node backend to the browser side of the monolith, client/src/main.tsx is the Vite/React entry point — it mounts the App component into the DOM root under StrictMode and imports the global stylesheet. client/index.html is the actual HTML file Vite serves that loads main.tsx as an ES module, completing the mirror image of server/index.ts on the frontend.

tour.title: The Sessions Monitor View
tour.description: client/src/App.tsx renders the top-level SectionTabs switch and shows either SessionsView or the lazily-loaded Management chunk based on the persisted section. SessionsView.tsx is the original live monitor: it owns the 3-second polling hook (useSessions.ts, calling the /api/sessions route from Step 3) and composes Header, Toolbar, and SessionList into the dashboard you actually watch.

tour.title: Rendering and Filtering Sessions
tour.description: SessionList.tsx and SessionRow.tsx render the actual list — each row shows the status dot, project/branch/model, and a context-usage bar, expanding into SessionDetail.tsx to show the subagent data from Step 7. Toolbar.tsx and filterSort.ts implement the filtering/sorting layer above them: filterSort.ts is pure domain logic (the View type and applyView function) that Toolbar's multi-select facets and sort controls drive, kept separate from rendering so the filter logic can be reasoned about and tested on its own.
tour.languageLesson: filterSort.ts separates pure state-transformation logic from the React components that display it — a common pattern where components stay declarative (just render props) while the actual filter/sort algorithm lives in a plain, framework-free function that's trivial to unit test.

tour.title: The Management Config Browser
tour.description: Alongside the live monitor, the Management tab gives a read-only, three-pane view of all Claude Code configuration on the machine. server/lib/management.ts is the backend scanner: it walks global (~/.claude, including installed plugins) and per-project (.claude/*) scopes to produce ScopeConfig data, plus a security allowlist that the file-serving endpoint checks against so arbitrary paths can never be read. client/src/components/management/ManagementView.tsx is its frontend counterpart, wiring ScopeMenu, ItemList, and DetailPane into the three-pane layout.

tour.title: Rendering Config Files
tour.description: DetailPane.tsx picks the right renderer for whatever config entry is selected — HookDetail.tsx for hook definitions, MarkdownViewer.tsx for Skill/agent docs (which splits YAML frontmatter into a table using shared/frontmatter.ts from Step 4's contract layer), or the generic FileViewer.tsx for everything else. This three-way dispatch is why the Management section can show wildly different file types (hooks, markdown, JSON, plain text) through one consistent detail pane.

tour.title: Containerized Deployment
tour.description: The Dockerfile packages the whole monolith into an image using four stages: deps installs pnpm dependencies once, dev bind-mounts the source for hot-reload, build bundles the Vite client into client/dist, and runtime is the minimal final image that runs the server via tsx with no compile step — mirroring the 'no daemon, no hooks, no compile' philosophy from the README. docker-compose.yml and docker-compose.dev.yml wire the production and development variants respectively, both mounting the host's ~/.claude read-only so the containerized dashboard can scan real session transcripts.
tour.languageLesson: Multi-stage Docker builds use one FROM per stage and COPY --from=<stage> to pull only needed artifacts forward. Here, the runtime stage never installs devDependencies or keeps the full source tree that the build stage needed — only the compiled client, the server source (run directly via tsx, not compiled), and node_modules from the shared deps stage.

tour.title: Project Documentation and History
tour.description: Beyond the README, .claude/CLAUDE.md is the living architecture reference this whole tour has been cross-checked against, covering the session-status state machine, usage-limit fetching, and the Management section in depth. docs/plans/2026-07-06-usage-token-refresh-removal.md documents a feature (an in-app OAuth token 'Sync' button) that was built and then deliberately removed — a useful capstone showing that server/lib/usage.ts's fail-open, no-refresh design from Step 8 was a considered decision, not an oversight.
